response.justification: While Bogdan brings relevant geospatial domain exposure through GISCollective and Green Map, and has distributed systems experience from high-throughput work at Dunnhumby, the role demands production-grade C++ or Rust expertise as its core requirement. His C++ usage is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project; it does not appear in his skills list, and no Rust experience is mentioned. His primary commercial stack for the past decade is TypeScript/Node.js, which is a fundamental mismatch for a pipeline engineering role where C++ proficiency is non-negotiable.
strengths: Geospatial domain experience via GISCollective (open-source geo-data platform) and Green Map GIS application development
Distributed systems exposure handling millions of HTTP requests per second using async I/O and low-latency patterns at Dunnhumby
Demonstrated range and learning agility across embedded, mobile, web, and infrastructure work over a 15+ year career
concerns: C++ experience is minimal, dated (2014–2015 at NI), and not in routing or navigation contexts; no Rust experience mentioned anywhere
Commercial work for the past 6+ years is exclusively TypeScript/Node.js — a fundamental stack mismatch for a C++-primary pipeline role
No experience with routing tile generation, navigation data pipelines, traffic signal integration, or production-scale map infrastructure
